item.content:encoded: Yay, I made it! I'm so happy to see people are still enjoying this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending reminds me of the Anti-Monitor from Crisis on Infinite Earths. (The comics not the CW shows)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is honestly one of my favorites I've read here. I love the layers to it and the "testimony of the last witness of the end" concept, and above all I love the idea of a Thing living between the fabric of reality working its way through the multiverse and slowly eating them up, and that manifesting as a hypermassive black hole. Great combo of Lovecraftian horror and astronomic awe. Bravo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would suggest not trusting that "ye olde english" converter; reading through what it did to those poor defenseless words was almost physically painful.
Wow, this is really spectacular. A dark, spooky read mixed with a wonderfully organic format screw and a bunch of VERY cool worldbuilding. I loved this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this idea, super creative, scary and funny. I really liked the reference to SCP—J. Only one complaint thought and that's that I don't think black holes can have a doppler shift because they can't emit light, but despite that it's still an easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, I love stories that play with the anti-meme concept and this does it nicely while making it part of an overall story. Some monster/phenomenon from outside the multiverse it going to drag us to it's domain of the unknown and it's real intimidating (reminds me a bit of SCP-3125 in that way (not that that's a bad thing)), done extra well with the post-apocalypse account from UNI-ZERO.
Each universe was super interesting to itself (I particular like the one that writes in unintelligible dots), while slowly adding to the information that pushes a sense of dread. Extreme mass dementia was a real interesting end-of-the-world scenario, I don't know if that's been done before (it might have). Plus it was packaged in a dynamic, interactive format screw that does your html/css skills service.

Some small complaints: I too thought the O5 override would be important, and then it wasn't, and I was… mildly disappointed. Not catastrophic though.
Also, that mary sue universe at the end was just… wat? It was kinda funny but it really broke the mood of the article for me. It's a good thing it's only readable after story mode is done, but honestly I wouldn't have it at all; too lolFoundation.

And finally, I don't know if this is a complaint or an avenue of possibility, but I feel there was some untapped potential right at the very end. I'm all for leaving the threat as an unknown, but the one thing that bugged me was whether The Maw was alive or not. Either one would be fine, but somehow bringing it up then not even hinting at which it might be was slightly disappointing.

All in all though, strong +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems to be an SCP-055 on steroids.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Originally I wrote that section with "hath", but to make sure I hadn't missed any typical word conversions, I ran the whole thing through some "ye olde english" converter I found online, then fixed any parts it broke. I guess I just didn't notice what it did with all the "has't" parts! My bad!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. The amount of "has't" in the medieval section that should be "have" or "hath" or literally just "hast" why is there an apostrophe is driving me up the wall, but dado helped make everything better.
Yay! One of my articles reached +100! I did it, scp-wiki!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The pacing in this is great, the story gave me chills and the coding is impressive, to say the least. I wish there were more creative usages of the medium like this on the site. Definite +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Check the author post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An absolutely fantastic SCP, but I feel like I'm missing something with the O5 override; no matter how much I try, I can't find the password. Could someone fill me in on what I'm missing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, you did a really good job in doing all the code and the scp had substance.

Also there are some interesting universes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, that was the intent!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if the SCP dado sent us was SCP-___-J? lol that would be hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for your kind words!

I wanted to put the dado part in there as a sort-of mid-story comic relief break, but maybe I timed it a bit poorly. There used to be another comic relief part in there, in the form of the Mary Sue universe, but I removed that from the story due to a lot of complaints. It's still in the list at the end though. I thought the dado one fit fine, but maybe I was wrong!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Clicking through the different iterations, combined with your amazing writing and well-paced exposition telling us what we're up against, created a very real sense of dread for me. But right when that sinking realization was at its strongest, well…

While I enjoy Dado in general, and I enjoy the idea of a Foundation by Dado, the appearance of his universe was somewhat jarring at that particular moment. It took me out of the story. It's funny by itself, but as a part of the article as a whole I feel like it distracts and detracts. It's still a huge +1 for me for your incredible writing, concept, formatting, and artwork.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No need to say sorry! I was worried that I might get +1's just because of the complexity of the code and such, and whilst I believe in rewarding hard work, I would rather get +1'd for people actually liking the story. So thank you for actually letting me know what brought it down! Hopefully, I can combat that if I do any format screws in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I couldn't really think of any better way to hide the O5 thing. Initially, it was open all the time but I thought that it had that 'looming but meaning nothing' feel you described, so I tried to make it look less significant by throwing it in a collapsible, and I had no complaints. I guess that wasn't enough though!

Maybe I shouldn't have added it at all, really. I just didn't want to annoy people that wanted to find a specific iteration by forcing them through the whole story, and that was the best way I could think to counter that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Formatting is pretty great the rest of it made me go meh. So no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this formatting is impressive, but everything stands out too much on its own to be interesting as a whole. novote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mhm. This isn't doing it for me, unfortunately. You've got parts that are funny (dado and past!Foundation), and they clash massively with the overall tone of the thing. I understand it's meant to be a lot of severely different universes, but it reads like a parody of the multiverse concept, where everything's got it's own clear 'theme'. And I wouldn't mind that if you embraced the silliness, but despite your jokes you still seem to be shooting for something big and serious, and it just misses the mark. Technologically it's very well-done, but story-wise it just has a lot of jarring themes and clashing tones, and kinda… leaves a lot go be desired, on the narrative front.

Sorry. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The O5 over-ride was honestly disappointing, It's looming through this whole thing and ends up not meaning anything really. All in all the "build up" structure of the page kinda works against it in the end since it never really caps to anything but before that it's very good at slowly describing what the entity can do indirectly. Other than those little things this is simultaneously one of the funniest pages and a nightmare.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[Inspirational quote, lyric, or poem here]

Boring image/font legal stuff...
Yaaaawn

Images:
Medieval Parchment Background (darkened slightly): https://pixabay.com/en/paper-parchment-thin-structure-539776/
Medieval Star Chart (section of): https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/3/3d/Alexander_Jamieson_Celestial_Atlas-Plate_1.jpg
Future Black Hole image (cropped): https://images.nasa.gov/details-GSFC_20171208_Archive_e000984.html
UNI-ZERO "Black Hole" image (edited): https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Laser-Induced_Optical_Damage_in_LiNbO3.jpg

Fonts:
Medieval Sharp Font: https://fonts.google.com/specimen/MedievalSharp
WC Rhesus B Bta (Ink Spots) Font: https://www.dafont.com/wc-rhesus-b-bta.font

Pretty sure it says they're all free-to-use or creative commons. I'll switch them out if needed.

Now for the fun stuff!
Or not, fine, be that way, jeez...

The O5 admin thingy at the bottom! It actually has TWO passwords, but they're really just 'cheat codes' for people coming back for a re-read that want to skip what I call "story mode".

I advise you only use these passwords after getting to the end naturally:
1. "forgetmenot" will open up the entire UNI list from the start.
2. "iremember" will open the list up AND take you to "UNI-ZERO" which isn't available on the list.

I reiterate: Please don't use them until you've seen it through properly at least once!

Next topic: I sort of want this to be a collaborative. You'll notice there's a universe in the list at the end that doesn't appear in the story. That's because this baby can handle universes that don't appear in the story and are just part of the normal universe list. However… you kind of need to know HTML/CSS to pull it off!

For those that have some web dev skills and are interested, you can Ctrl+F through the code for some sections I've ingeniously labeled "STEP ONE", "STEP TWO", and "STEP THREE". They guide you through the process. Of course, you should probably try adding this article's code to your own sandbox to ensure your attempted integration actually works!

Thanks to Cyantreuse, Rounderhouse, XilasCrowe and Gummy_Dragon for feedback/criticism/advice/etc!
Also thanks to… I think it was Prototype_Toaster and Croquembouche for confirming the concept was interesting when I first started. I think it was those two… it was like, 1.5 months ago, my memory sucks.

If I forgot you somehow, lemme know!
